one_line_thesis: Global short-term rental platform with $4.6B FY2025 FCF and durable nights growth; at ~$147 the base return (~14%/yr) is below accumulate hurdle — watch.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
Airbnb is the leading global short-term rental platform: 533 million nights booked in FY2025, $91.3 billion GBV, and $4.6 billion of free cash flow. Q1 2026 accelerated with revenue +18% and nights +9%. Adjusted EBITDA was $4.3 billion in FY2025 (36% margin) and $519 million in Q1 2026 (19% margin, seasonally low).

At about $147 per share (Yahoo Finance, 2026-07-10), the Lawrence base case implies about 14.1% per year on normalized owner cash of $7.38 per share, below our 15% accumulate hurdle. The business has a stable durable advantage (Munger moat) on brand and network scale, with parti...

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

valuation_decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

timeline.label: Net income moved +81.0% versus the prior period (2,648.0 to 4,792.0).
Leadership / governance risk elevated. Airbnb (ABNB) CFO Elinor Mertz sells 3,750 shares under 10b5-1 plan - Stock Titan; 71 executive sales ($177,776,563) in 120d
Q1 2026: revenue +18% to $2.7B; Nights and Seats Booked 156M (+9%); GBV $29.2B; Adjusted EBITDA $519M (19% margin); FCF $1.7B
FY2025 10-K: revenue $12.2B (+10%); nights 533M (+8%); GBV $91.3B (+12%); FCF $4.6B; net income $2.5B; Adjusted EBITDA $4.3B
Repurchased 29.7M Class A shares for $3.8B in 2025; $5.6B remaining under August 2025 authorization
Board approved new $6.0B share repurchase program (August 2025)
February 2024 share repurchase program authorized; completed in 2025
Expanded brand platform beyond core stays into experiences and services; KPI renamed Nights and Seats Booked
Post-pandemic travel recovery drove GBV and nights re-acceleration after 2020–2021 disruption
IPO on Nasdaq (ABNB); dual-class structure with founder super-voting shares
COVID-19 shock: global nights collapsed; company cut costs and later benefited from domestic and long-term-stay demand
International expansion scaled; multi-region GBV mix reduced single-city concentration risk
Founded in San Francisco as marketplace connecting hosts and guests for short-term stays

industry.structure: Global short-term rental is oligopolistic at the platform layer (Airbnb, Booking, Vrbo/Expedia) with fragmented supply (millions of hosts). Hotels and metasearch remain parallel distribution. Regulatory lodging taxes and local short-term rental bans are the main structural frictions.
industry.share_shift: Airbnb is gaining nights and gross booking value (+8% nights, +12% GBV in FY2025) with strength across all regions; experiences and services remain a small mix versus stays but management is investing for attach.
industry.trend: AI-assisted search and trip planning, long-term stays (28+ nights), and cross-border travel normalization are current growth drivers; cities continue tightening STR rules and lodging-tax collection, which raises compliance cost but also barriers for small entrants.
